old_claim.note: Theater Ticketing Systems provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency remains because theater ticketing is an advertised event-commerce category, not a prerequisite for paid notices in newspapers.

source_shape.source_locator: MHS anchors the first paid newspaper advertisements to Boston News-Letter issue number three dated 1-8 May 1704 and lists stolen goods, lost anvils, and real estate as the early notices.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source identifies paid newspaper notices before any theater-ticketing relationship, so performance ticketing is not the source-backed dependency.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Theater performances can be advertised in newspapers, but the target technology is the paid newspaper-notice channel itself, which does not require theater admission systems.

ontology_before: Public Newspaper Advertising depended directly on Theater Ticketing Systems, implying performance admission systems enabled newspaper advertising.
ontology_after: Public Newspaper Advertising is scoped to the newspaper paid-notice channel, with performance ads treated as later content rather than prerequisite infrastructure.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: public_newspaper_advertising no longer directly depends on theater_ticketing_systems.
would_reject_if: The node is later narrowed specifically to theater-performance advertisements in newspapers.
A stronger source shows theater ticketing systems were necessary for the first paid newspaper-advertising model.
A separate theater-advertising node is added where theater_ticketing_systems is the relevant scoped prerequisite.
short_reason: Theater ticketing is a content/use-case category, not the enabling paid-newspaper-notice mechanism.
